verse_specific_anchors: the explicit chapter-closing function — eṣā ('this') as the back-reference over the entire chapter from 2.11 to 2.71 — is preserved verbatim in the engineering layer ('eṣā — this — refers back over the chapter's whole argument from 2.11 to 2.71'); no other verse in the chapter has this back-reference function

the brāhmī sthiti and brahma-nirvāṇa technical terms are unique to this verse in the chapter (and brāhmī-sthiti specifically is a terminus-naming move that appears here for the first time in the Gita); the engineering layer correctly identifies them as the verse's defining technical contribution

the anta-kāle 'pi structural force (even at the end-time, brief establishment suffices) is preserved as 'Even brief establishment carries through to the closing scenes of a career'; this anti-deathbed-anxiety force is unique to 2.72
objection: The analog could not equally apply to 2.55 (Arjuna's question opening the sthita-prajña block), 2.58 (the tortoise verse), 2.61 (the prior pratiṣṭhitā formulation), 2.70 (the ocean), or 2.71 (the four-fold compound). All those verses describe the steady-mind state structurally; only 2.72 names the chapter-closing terminus and the anta-kāle 'pi structural force. The 'twenty-five years in' framing is licensed by the verse's specific terminus-naming function — the engineering layer's depiction of a long-career senior is appropriate to the chapter's terminal verse, not transferable to the chapter's earlier verses. Pass.
